notes: PREPARATION FOR THE MINOR All East Asian Studies minors are expected to gain proficiency in either Chinese or Japanese language. Transfer students are strongly encouraged to complete one of the following courses, or an articulated equivalent, prior to transfer: CHIN 1: First-Year Chinese JAPN 1: First-Year Japanese Students with prior knowledge of Chinese or Japanese are required to complete a language placement exam to determine which UCSC course of Chinese or Japanese language instruction best suits their skill level. Instructions for the placement exam may be found on the Languages and Applied Linguistics website . Students may also pursue study abroad opportunities as a way of acquiring Chinese or Japanese language instruction. Student may additionally choose to complete one of the following courses prior to transferring: HIS 40A: Early Modern East Asia HIS 40B: The Making of Modern East Asia Please visit the East Asian Studies website to learn more about this minor.

UC Santa Cruz students may choose to complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and allowable credits. While a minor will appear on a student's official transcript, it will not be listed on their diploma. Pursuing a minor is a personal decision that should align with a student's educational and career goals. Students do not apply for minors when submitting their application to UC Santa Cruz. After enrolling, transfer students interested in pursuing a minor should contact the department offering the minor for guidance.
